notes: Commander of horse under Hicetes, noted for bravery and courage in Plutarch's account.

docs.title: Life of Timoleon
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … and traitors; and Euthymus, though a brave man, and one of singular courage, could obtain no mercy, because he was charged with contemptuous language in disparagement of the Corinthians when they first sent their forces into Sicily: it is said that he told the Leontini in a speech, that the
